items.name: The Coca-Cola Company
items.headline: KO North America President Mann stepping down; CFO Murphy interim
whatChanged: Jennifer Mann stepping down as EVP & President, North America Operating Unit effective Aug 1, 2026
President & CFO John Murphy to lead North America Operating Unit on interim basis
Mann to remain as senior advisor through April 2027 for transition
Permanent successor for North America President to be named at a later date
items.decisionTakeaway: Leadership transition at KO's largest operating unit introduces near-term execution risk, though CFO Murphy's interim role provides continuity while a permanent successor is sought.

items.name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
items.headline: JPM names Petno & Rohrbaugh Co-Presidents; Lake to retire
whatChanged: Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh, Co-CEOs of CIB, elected Co-Presidents of JPM effective immediately; Petno becomes sole CEO of CIB, Rohrbaugh becomes CEO of CCB
Marianne Lake, current CEO of CCB, retiring after 25+ years; will assist with transition
CMDC approved one-time retention RSU awards: $30M each to Petno and Rohrbaugh, $20M each to Mary Erdoes and Jennifer Piepszak
Retention awards cliff-vest after 3 years subject to 12% three-year average ROTCE (2026-2028) and continuous employment; no retirement vesting
Prior comparable 8-K (filed 2026-06-24) covered DFAST stress test results only — no leadership changes
items.decisionTakeaway: This is a significant succession-planning milestone signaling the next generation of JPM leadership, with $100M in retention awards tying top executives to a 12% ROTCE hurdle through 2028.

items.name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
items.headline: JPM Dividend Hike to $1.65 and New $50B Buyback
whatChanged: Quarterly dividend intended to increase to $1.65/share from $1.50, effective Q3 2026
Board authorized new $50B common share repurchase program effective July 1, 2026
SCB requirement unchanged at 2.5% through Sept 30, 2027; CET1 requirement remains 11.5%
Prior comparable filing was a $500M debt note offering — no capital return actions
items.decisionTakeaway: JPM signals strong capital return capacity with a 10% dividend hike and a fresh $50B buyback, supported by unchanged regulatory capital requirements.

items.name: NVIDIA Corporation
items.headline: NVIDIA prices 7-tranche senior notes totaling ~$25B+
whatChanged: NVIDIA is establishing terms for 7 series of senior notes due 2028–2056 under its 2016 indenture, with coupons ranging from 4.250% to 5.625%
Visible principal amounts: $3.5B (2028 Notes), $3.5B (2029 Notes), $4.0B (2031 Notes); remaining tranches (2033, 2036, 2046, 2056) not fully shown but coupons are 4.750%, 4.950%, 5.550%, 5.625%
Notes are optionally redeemable at Treasury Rate + 5 bps (make-whole) prior to maturity/par call date; not convertible, not mandatorily redeemable
Officers' Certificate signed by CFO Colette Kress and VP/Treasurer Chris Ginieczki certifying indenture compliance for all 7 tranches
This is a new debt issuance — no comparable debt offering was in the prior Q1 FY27 earnings 8-K
items.decisionTakeaway: NVIDIA is tapping the investment-grade bond market with a large multi-tranche offering to fund its aggressive AI infrastructure buildout and shareholder returns, adding meaningful long-term debt to a historically under-levered balance sheet.
